evals.prompt: Review my Python API changes. I added a new user management module with CRUD endpoints and email notifications.
evals.expected_output: A structured code review identifying SQL injection vulnerabilities, hardcoded secrets, missing authentication, password in plaintext, path traversal, swallowed exceptions, and SRP violations. Should use the severity levels P0-P3 and the expected output format.
expectations: Review identifies SQL injection in at least 3 endpoints
Review flags the hardcoded API key (ADMIN_API_KEY) as P0 or P1
Review identifies missing authentication on CRUD endpoints
Review flags plaintext password storage and logging
Review identifies path traversal vulnerability in export_user_data
Review flags the swallowed exception in the email sending block
Review uses the P0-P3 severity format from the skill
Review includes an overall assessment (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT)
Review includes a Next Steps section with options
Review identifies the password being sent in the welcome email

evals.prompt: Review this TypeScript PR. It's a new OrderService that handles order creation, payment, inventory management, and caching.
evals.expected_output: A structured review identifying SRP violation (god class), N+1 query patterns in getUserOrders, race conditions between payment/inventory, missing null checks, SQL injection in getOrdersByStatus, == vs === comparison, and the dead exportOrders method.
expectations: Review identifies SRP violation - OrderService handles too many concerns
Review flags N+1 query pattern in getUserOrders (nested API calls per order per item)
Review identifies race condition: payment charged before inventory reserved
Review flags SQL injection in getOrdersByStatus (string interpolation with parameterized query syntax mismatch)
Review identifies missing null check on getOrder result (result.rows[0] could be undefined)
Review flags == instead of === in cancelOrder status comparison
Review uses the P0-P3 severity format from the skill
Review includes an overall assessment (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT)
Review mentions the dead exportOrders method or unused legacy getOrdersByStatus
Review identifies that notification failure is silently swallowed

evals.prompt: Check my Go changes for issues. I wrote a session manager with in-memory storage for our HTTP service.
evals.expected_output: A structured review identifying race conditions (unlocked map access in CreateSession, cleanupLoop, UpdateSession, ImportSessions), path traversal in Export/ImportSessions, global mutable state, TOCTOU in DeleteSession, dead code (migrateV1Sessions, BulkCleanup), and unvalidated JSON unmarshal errors.
expectations: Review identifies race condition: CreateSession accesses sm.sessions without holding the lock
Review identifies race condition: cleanupLoop iterates map without lock
Review identifies race condition: UpdateSession accesses session without lock
Review flags path traversal vulnerability in ExportSessions/ImportSessions
Review identifies the global mutable activeCount variable as unsafe for concurrent access
Review flags ignored json.Unmarshal errors (CreateSession, ImportSessions)
Review identifies dead code: migrateV1Sessions and/or BulkCleanup
Review uses the P0-P3 severity format from the skill
Review includes an overall assessment (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT)
Review identifies TOCTOU in DeleteSession (check-then-act with lock release in between)
